other_observers: 12/22/05 Glenn Walbeck, Lock Kilpatrick, and Nick Komar. 12/23/05 Bill Schmoker (both times), Andrew Spencer (both times), Joey Kellner (2nd time), and Nathan Pieplow (2nd time).; Mark Peterson originally found the bird a few hours before I saw the bird on 12/22/05.
specific_location: On the south side of Bonny Reservoir. Drive into Bonny SP and then follow the road west along the reservoir until it ends. It was in the row of trees and bushes on the south west side of where the road ends.
description: Brown head, back, and tail. A white spot at the base of the primaries and a little bit of white on the secondaries. Rufous color on the side of the body and the undertail covert. White belly. Red eye.Behaviors: Very Towhee like. It stayed in thick cover most of the time while we were trying to find and while we were looking at it. Occasionally it would get in the open, but when it did it would usually fly to a new location.Call: A nasal zewheekPlumage: Adult
photographer: Photographer: Cole Wild, Cole Wild, Cole Wild, Cole Wild
